design_principles: Surface uncertainty, never hide it — missing, unclear, and conflicting data must be visible at a glance, not buried.
Every AI assertion is traceable to a vendor evidence snippet; nothing is taken on faith.
Comparison supports the buyer's decision; it never declares a winner or implies false precision.
Vendors that aren't yet comparable are visibly separated from those that are, so the buyer doesn't compare apples to gaps.
Action over analysis: every gap the tool finds should lead to a concrete next step (a clarification to send).
what_buyer_sees_first: A status banner: how many vendors responded, how many are 'comparable now' vs 'need clarification', and the single biggest cross-vendor risk — before any prices.

screens.name: RFQ Overview
screens.purpose: Ground the buyer in exactly what was asked, so vendor gaps are judged against a clear baseline.

ux_copy.heading: What this procurement event is asking for
ux_copy.empty_state: No RFQ generated yet. Generate one to begin.

screens.how_risk_is_surfaced: Compliance and per-item-pricing expectations are highlighted as 'will be checked' so the buyer knows which vendor gaps matter most.

screens.name: Vendor Upload
screens.purpose: Bring vendor responses into the system by paste or file, or load the generated sample set.

ux_copy.heading: Add vendor responses
ux_copy.empty_state: No vendor responses yet. Paste, upload, or load the sample set.

screens.how_risk_is_surfaced: Inputs are shown raw and unedited so the buyer can confirm the AI is reading the real submission, not a cleaned-up version.

screens.name: Extraction Review
screens.purpose: Show what the AI pulled from each vendor, with evidence, and flag every gap and conflict.

ux_copy.heading: What each vendor actually said
ux_copy.empty_state: Run extraction to see structured results.

screens.how_risk_is_surfaced: Colour-coded status chips (present / missing / unclear / conflicting) on every field, plus a dedicated flags panel; values without evidence can never show as 'present'.

screens.name: Vendor Comparison
screens.purpose: Help the buyer see differences and decide what to do next — without false precision.

ux_copy.heading: How the vendors compare
ux_copy.empty_state: Run comparison once extraction is complete.

screens.how_risk_is_surfaced: Dimensions that can't be fairly compared are labelled 'Not comparable yet' with the reason, instead of being scored; confidence is shown per cell.

screens.name: Prompt Trace
screens.purpose: Make the AI workflow auditable — show input, prompt, raw model output, and final structured output.

ux_copy.heading: How the AI reached this
ux_copy.empty_state: Run any agent to capture a trace.

screens.how_risk_is_surfaced: Full transparency: the buyer (or an auditor) can see the exact prompt and the exact model output behind any displayed result.

comparison_view_design.layout: Vendors as columns, dimensions as rows. A banner above the matrix states overall comparability before any cell is read.
comparison_view_design.how_non_comparable_vendors_are_shown: A grey 'Not yet comparable' badge on the vendor and on any dimension where data gaps prevent a fair call, with the blocking reason inline.
comparison_view_design.how_evidence_is_exposed: Every assessment cell links back to the extraction evidence snippet that supports it; cells with low confidence are visibly marked.
